name: Quinshon Judkins
headline: Workhorse usage masks a rookie efficiency problem
body:
Judkins handled 230 carries across 14 games as a rookie and finished the RB24, but the surface counting stats hide some warts. He averaged a thin 3.6 yards per carry, posted a negative -0.126 EPA per play, and forced a broken tackle just once every 20.9 attempts, gaining only 2.2 yards after contact. The volume was real, though: 33 red-zone opportunities and 11 goal-line carries turned into seven scores, and four runs of 30-plus yards show the home-run gear is there.

The receiving role barely exists, with a 6.9% target share, 26 grabs, and 0.96 yards per route run capping his PPR ceiling. He's recovering from a Week 16 ankle and fibula fracture but tracking toward the opener. A rebuilt line should lift the efficiency floor. Bank on a 300-touch projection and draft him as a high-volume RB2 with real touchdown equity.
